meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Slow Dev Speed
content.seo_description: Learn how to optimize your analytics setup and overcome slow development speed. An Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from a lack of visibility into user friction. By implementing event-driven analytics early, you transform guesswork into data-backed iterations. Using Antigravity to automate instrumentation and Claude Code to refactor bottlenecked logic, you can reduce feedback loops from days to hours, accelerating your project velocity significantly.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a codebase that feels like a labyrinth. You’ve spent weeks building features, yet you have no idea if users are actually clicking your buttons or dropping off in frustration. This 'blind flying' is the primary culprit behind sluggish development velocity; without data, you’re just guessing, and guessing is the slowest way to build software.

But what if you could visualize user behavior as clearly as you see your own code? By integrating a robust analytics layer into your architecture, you stop building in a vacuum. This guide provides the blueprint for students to move from amateur guesswork to engineering precision, utilizing high-velocity tools to ship faster and smarter.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the three key events that signal user success. This prevents data bloat and keeps your instrumentation focused on high-value interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project architecture and suggest a schema for three core user engagement metrics that correlate with long-term retention.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Identify the top 3 user events in my codebase that indicate successful onboarding and suggest an event tracking naming convention.'

steps.title: Automate Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Stop manually adding loggers. Use Antigravity to inject analytics hooks into your event handlers automatically, ensuring consistent data collection across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable tracking utility function that wraps my primary API calls and button handlers to fire analytics events automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement a centralized analytics wrapper in src/utils/tracking.ts that integrates with my existing event handlers.'

steps.title: Debug Instrumentation with Claude Code
steps.description: Ensure your events are firing correctly by using Claude Code to simulate user sessions and verify the data payload in the console and network tab.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that simulates user navigation and verifies that the analytics events are captured with the correct metadata.

steps.title: Analyze Bottleneck Latency
steps.description: Correlate analytics data with performance metrics to find exactly where your app is slowing down for the end user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my application logs and event data to identify the top three user flows that exhibit the highest latency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Find the slowest API routes based on current event logs and suggest code refactoring to improve response times.'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Data Insights
steps.description: Use the insights gathered to pivot your development roadmap, focusing only on the features that directly improve your North Star metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the recent event logs, suggest a high-impact UI change that would reduce user drop-off in the signup flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the signup component to include the suggested UI changes to improve conversion rate.'

pro_tips: Never track everything; focus on 'actionable' events that lead to a specific product decision.
Use pseudonymous User IDs early; it makes debugging specific user pain points exponentially easier.
Implement 'heartbeat' events to measure session duration, which is a leading indicator of engagement.
Always sanitize your event payloads to ensure no sensitive PII is accidentally sent to your analytics provider.
Automate your analytics tests in CI/CD to ensure new features don't break your tracking layer.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, schedule a weekly 'Data Review' session where you look at your dashboard for 30 minutes before planning your next sprint.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start building with the precision of a senior engineer. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap—now it’s time to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
